Fun stuff. Quite sick and hungover. Send all the uplifting things.
item.dc:creator: deafkitten

item.title: What a fucking legend
item.description: https://apnews.com/article/dolly-parton-childrens-hospital-philanthropy-4bceb89b8e6fd4f22bdf96f1861d504d I know the title national treasure gets thrown around a lot but Dolly truly deserves that title. While other billionaires bitch and moan about going to space and making AI robot butlers, Dolly is out there making sure the people of Tennessee (and beyond) don’t have to sell their organs to get access to healthcare for their kids. In a world full of Bezos’ and Elons, we need more Dollys.
item.dc:creator: DownvotesStarWars

item.title: Meme Redistribution Centre TM
item.description: Buttella
item.dc:creator: HuskyHairExtraordinaire

item.title: Sunday Wholesome Dump Of Smile Marshmallows (To Warm The Heart) #384
item.description:
1. Bro kept it real
2. Wholesome caricature :)
3. RUNNNN
4. I love how randomly happy they are :)
5. Just a dad and his duck
6. More homies like this!
7
8. CHEESE
9. Bro they are playing proposal trap cards and stuff
10. Aww
11
12
13. There are still nice people in this world, Mr. Frodo
14. It's YOU, reading this :)
15. Thanks for scrolling all the way trough! Hope this dump made you happy! Finally I would like to share with you a little passion project of mine. Me and my brother are making a game that is inspired by the book Obedience to Authority as well as media such as Papers, Please and the show Severance. We are from Eastern Europe and kind of in the middle between East and West and the subject of authoritarianism and why people just blindly follow orders has always been interesting to me. Whatever prejudice some media might have against people in Eastern Europe, I assure you we are some of the hardest working and welcoming people you will ever meet! If you can please check out and wishlist our game! We are also committed to not using any generative AI in the game. No AI art, no AI music, no AI scripts, no AI voices! https://store.steampowered.com/app/2719670/The_Milgram_Experiment/
item.dc:creator: TheseusGames

item.title: Jayme Lawson holds nothing back when asked how she felt regarding the BAFTAs controversy
item.description: Considering it was her "SINNERS" co-stars Michael B. Jordan and Delroy Lindo who had to endure getting the N-word shouted at them while on stage without warning. Then to have the BAFTAs and BBC refuse to censor the incident. They did it for a disgusting ratings stunt, and Lawson calls them out (while also specifing that the man with tourettes was also exploited).
item.dc:creator: Pasquinelthe2nd

item.title: What a weird way to say that one party is holding people accountable
item.description: TLDR: Democrats: “In the past week, institutions have been quick to cut ties with several former Democratic officials because of their interactions with Epstein.” Republicans: “Top Republicans with ties to Epstein, including President Trump, Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick and former Trump adviser Steve Bannon, have faced political blowback from items in the files. But they haven’t severed ties with institutions or otherwise pulled back from their powerful perches. Trump said to reporters Friday that he was “fully exonerated” in the Epstein case and suggested that Lutnick would be willing to speak to the committee if asked. “He’s a very innocent guy,” Trump said” If you’re inclined to read the rest: https://apple.news/AaNBGlNweQQuczIFWfu6xoA
item.dc:creator: DownvotesStarWars

item.title: The Resistance Has Begun - Across the US, people are dismantling and destroying Flock surveillance cameras. Anger over ICE connections and privacy violations is fueling the sabotage.
item.description: Last week, in La Mesa, a small city just east of San Diego, California, observers happened upon a pair of destroyed Flock cameras. One had been smashed and left on the median, the other had key parts removed. The destruction was obviously intentional, and appears perhaps even staged to leave a message: It came just weeks after the city decided, in the face of public protest, to continue its contracts with the surveillance company. Flock cameras are typically mounted on 8 to 12 foot poles and powered by a solar panel. The smashed remains of all of the above in La Mesa are the latest examples of a widening anti-Flock backlash. In recent months, people have been smashing and dismantling the surveillance devices, in incidents reported in at least five states, from coast to coast. Bill Paul, who runs the local news outlet San Diego Slackers, and who first reported on the smashed Flock equipment, tells me that the sabotage comes just a month or two after San Diego held a raucous city council meeting over whether to keep operating the Flock cameras. A clear majority of public attendees present were in favor of shutting them down. There was “a huge turnout against them,” he tells me, “but the council approved continuation of the contract.” The tenor of the meeting reflects a growing anger and concern over the surveillance technology that’s gone nationwide: Flock, which is based in Atlanta and is currently valued at $7.5 billion, operates automatic license plate readers (ALPR) that have now been installed in some 6,000 US communities. They gather not just license plate images, but other identifying data used to ‘fingerprint’ vehicles, their owners, and their movements. This data can be collected, stored, and accessed without a warrant, making it a popular workaround for law enforcement. Perhaps most controversially, Flock’s vehicle data is routinely accessed by ICE. If you’ve heard Flock’s name come up recently, it’s likely as a result of their now-canceled partnership with Ring, made instantly famous by a particularly dystopian Super Bowl ad that promised to turn regular neighborhoods into a surveillance dragnet. Meanwhile, abuses have been prevalent. A Georgia police chief was arrested and charged with using Flock data to stalk and harass private citizens. Flock data has been used to track citizens who cross state lines for abortions when the procedure is illegal in their state. And municipalities have found that federal agencies have accessed local flock data without their knowledge or consent. Critics claim that this warrantless data collection is Orwellian and unconstitutional; a violation of the 4th amendment. As a result, civilians from Oregon to Virginia to California and beyond are pushing their governments to abandon Flock contracts. In some cases, they’re succeeding. Cities like Santa Cruz, CA, and Eugene, OR, have cancelled their contracts with Flock. In Oregon’s case, the public outcry was accompanied by a campaign of destruction against the surveillance devices: Last year, at least six Flock license plate readers mounted on poles located in Eugene and Springfield were cut down and destroyed, according to the Lookout Eugene-Springfield. A note reading “Hahaha get wrecked ya surveilling fucks” was attached to one of the destroyed poles, and somewhat incredibly, broadcast on the local news. In Greenview, Illinois, a Flock camera pole was severed at the base and the device destroyed. In Lisbon, Connecticut, police are investigating another smashed Flock camera. In Virginia, last December, a man was arrested for dismantling and destroying 13 Flock cameras throughout the state over the course of the year. He’s apparently already admitted to doing so, according to local news: https://www.bloodinthemachine.com/p/across-the-us-people-are-dismantling DeFlock An open-source project mapping license plate readers. https://deflock.org/
item.dc:creator: Jbelkin

item.title: RAM Going Into Fake Datacenters That Literally Can’t Be Used
item.description: RAM. Flash storage. CPUs. Hard drives. Copper. All going into big boxes in the middle of nowhere where nobody can use them. Only when these developers go bust and start manually tearing them down and stripping them for parts, and even then the parts won't find their way into consumer products.

item.title: GovBrief Today #394
item.description: America First was always just a slogan. One head of state sits in a New York jail cell. Another was killed Saturday when U.S. and Israeli forces bombed the capital city where he lived. Donald Trump remains who he has always been. Tonight's most dangerous story is the reporting that surfaced about Michael Flynn, Trump's former national security adviser. Ten days ago Flynn gathered six federal officials and a room full of 2020 election deniers to discuss declaring a national emergency to seize control of this fall's midterms. The attendees left behind videos, photos, emails, and a LinkedIn post celebrating dinner with Flynn and Cleta Mitchell. Call or write your representatives and demand immediate public hearings into White House officials who met to discuss seizing our elections. We've emailed 6,300+ stories over 394 straight nights. The algorithm doesn't always show them to you. Subscribe now at govbrief.today #GovBriefToday #Resist Subscribe (It's Free!) - https://gbounacos.substack.com [1] https://go.govbrief.today/iran-war-feb28 [2] https://go.govbrief.today/us-backs-pakistan [3] https://go.govbrief.today/election-takeover-meeting [4] https://go.govbrief.today/opec-oil-crisis [5] https://go.govbrief.today/pentagon-ig-stalls [6] https://go.govbrief.today/dems-iran-vote [7] https://go.govbrief.today/noem-fleet [8] https://go.govbrief.today/roxbury-ice Subscribe (It's Free!) - https://gbounacos.substack.com
item.dc:creator: GeorgeBounacos

item.description: Well damn. Enjoyed the Hyperion Cantos tremendously. I will have to reread in his honor.
